revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猪上神社]] <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Expand Japanese|猪上神社}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Igami Shrine | native_name = 猪上神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Igami Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.60762|135.671432}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Igami Shrine'''|猪上神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
== instance of (P31) ==
* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}
== part of (P361) ==
* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 41 ** P155: {{ill|Q11483495|ltq=Q11483495|qid=Q11483495|1=Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q119929665|ltq=Q119929665|qid=Q119929665|1=Funayama Shrine}}
== located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) ==
* {{ill|Heguri, Nara|lt=Heguri|ar|هيغوري|arz|هيجورى|ceb|Heguri-chō|en|Heguri, Nara|eo|Heguri|es|Heguri (Nara)|fa|هگوری، نارا|fr|Heguri|hy|Հեգուրի|it|Heguri|ja|平群町|ko|헤구리정|ms|Heguri, Nara|my|ဟဲဂုရိမြို့၊ နာရ|pl|Heguri|ru|Хегури|simple|Heguri, Nara|sv|Heguri|tl|Heguri, Nara|tr|Heguri|uk|Хеґурі (Нара)|zh|平群町|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} * {{ill|Q907495|ltq=Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Q11483501|ltq=Q11483501|qid=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}
== coordinate location (P625) ==
* {{coord|34.60762|135.671432}}<ref>Kokugakuin University Shrine database</ref>
== Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) ==
* 180700 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}}
== country (P17) ==
* Мастор|my|ဂျပန်နိုင်ငံ|mzn|جاپون|nap|Giappone|nds_nl|Japan|nds|Japan|ne|जापान|new|जापान|nia|Jepang|nl|Japan|nlnews|Categorie:Japan|nlvoyage|Japan|nn|Japan|nov|Japan|no|Japan|nqo|ߗ߭ߊߔߐ߲߫|nrm|Japon|nr|Japane|nso|Japane|nv|Binááʼádaałtsʼózí Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}
== street address (P6375) ==
* {'text': '奈良県生駒郡平群町信貴畑字信貴山2260', 'language': 'ja'}
[[JA:猪上神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q119929666}}
== Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=September 2022}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Inoue Shrine | Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Ikoma District}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Shigibata, aza Shigisan 2260 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude d = 34 | Latitude m = 36 | Latitude s = 27 | Longitude d = 135 | Longitude m = 40 | Longitude s = 17.2 | Enshrined deities = Ame-tarashihiko-no-Mikoto / Kuni-oshihito-no-Mikoto | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} | Founded = | Main hall = | Pilgrimage sites = | Chief priest = | Other names = | Annual festival = | Rituals = | Map = Japan Nara }} '''Inoue Shrine''' (Igami Jinja) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shigibata, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, and is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.
=== Overview === It is located just over about 100 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|qid=Q11573|1=Metre}} past the Niō Gate of Shigisan {{ill|lt=Chōgosonshi-ji|Q11092571|qid=Q11092571|1=Chōgosonshi-ji}}, the head temple of the {{ill|lt=Shigisan Shingon sect|Q9393338|qid=Q9393338|1=Shigisan Shingon-shū}}, on the immediate right side of the approach path.
Since ancient times, it has stood within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Mount Shigi|Q6923605|qid=Q6923605|1=Mount Shigi}} and served as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|qid=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Chōgosonshi-ji.
It is said that the site of the {{ill|lt=sutra repository (architecture)|Q1325051|qid=Q1325051|lt=sutra repository|1=Kyōzō}} located between the water pavilion at the foot of the main hall stone steps and the Reihō Hall was the former shrine site. The shrine was widely revered under the name “Shigisan Igami Daimyōjin”<ref>{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|pages=661–664}}</ref>.
In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1870), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu bunri edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine was moved outside the [https://kotobank.jp/word/総門･惣門-2056818 sōmon (main gate)] to Mount Kengai. Later, in the 11th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}, it was relocated to its present position beside the approach path<ref>{{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|page=661–664}}</ref>.
The {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kuni-oshihito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|qid=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}. Within the main hall, Ame-tarashihiko-no-Mikoto is enshrined on the left side facing inward, and Kuni-oshihito-no-Mikoto on the right.
Although the origins are not clearly transmitted, the ''[https://kotobank.jp/word/阿娑縛抄-25051 Asabashō]'' from the second year of {{ill|lt=Kōan|Q389180|qid=Q389180|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}} records that during the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era, when Myōren, who revitalized Mount Shigi, prayed for the expansion of the temple buildings, a sacred boar appeared. Because the construction was completed overnight, it is said that this sacred boar was enshrined.
Mount Shigi was formerly called Mount Inoue, and one theory holds that “Inoue Shrine” derives from an {{ill|lt=ateji|Q754018|qid=Q754018|1=Ateji}} for Inoue. Another theory suggests that, based on the location of the former shrine site, an “Ogami Shrine” that worshipped the {{ill|lt=sacred peak|Q13479654|qid=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} Mount Shigi later underwent a change in pronunciation and was written using {{ill|lt=phonetic borrowing|Q10888308|qid=Q10888308|1=phonetic borrowing}}, becoming “Inoue Shrine.”
=== Access ===
=== By public transportation ===
* From {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Yamatoji Line|Q1151659|qid=Q1151659|1=Yamatoji Line}} “{{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|qid=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}},” take a {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|qid=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Shigisanmon, get off at Shigi Ōhashi, then about a 5-minute walk. (Also via Nara Kōtsū bus from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Ikoma Line|Q900218|qid=Q900218|1=Ikoma Line}} “{{ill|lt=Shigisan-shita Station|Q7496434|qid=Q7496434|1=Shigisanshita Station}}”) * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|qid=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}} “{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Q6379464|qid=Q6379464|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}},” take the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|qid=Q874150|lt=Shigi Line|1=Shigi Line}} to “{{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|qid=Q7496433|1=Shigisanguchi Station}},” transfer to the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nishi-Shigi funicular|Q8140289|qid=Q8140289|lt=cable car|1=Nishi-Shigi Cable Line}}, then transfer to a {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|qid=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} at “{{ill|lt=Takayasuyama Station|Q7677479|qid=Q7677479|1=Takayasuyama Station}},” and get off at Shigisanmon.
=== By private car ===
* Via the {{ill|lt=Hanna Road|Q11656692|qid=Q11656692|1=Hanna road}} from the Ikoma trailhead, then the {{ill|lt=Shigi-Ikoma Skyline|Q11386259|qid=Q11386259|1=Shigi-Ikoma Skyline}}. * Kashiwara City Road Shigi-Ōgata Line: From the Ōgata-minami intersection on {{ill|lt=National Route 170|Q6156852|qid=Q6156852|1=Japan National Route 170}} (old road) ({{ill|lt=Kashiwara City|Q873583|qid=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}), head east, pass the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takao (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11669240|qid=Q11669240|lt=Mount Takao|1=Mount Takao}}, the vicinity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shigisan Nodoka Village|1=UNKNOWN}}, and proceed to the area near Shigisanmon. * Via {{ill|lt=Nara Prefectural Route 236 Shigisan Line|Q11445341|qid=Q11445341|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 236}}. * Via {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefectural Route 183 Hondō-Takaida Line|Q11441300|qid=Q11441300|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 183}}. * Parking fee: 500 yen (passenger cars / once per day)
=== Notes === {{Notes help}} <references />
{{Shinto-stub}}
== Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Igami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Igami Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Chinjusha]]
